mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and PKC signaling to suppress proliferation in FLT3-driven AML models
additional_notes: Described as approved in AML; benchmark FLT3 pathway inhibitor; MOLM-13 often sensitive

mechanism: Potent inhibition of FLT3 (noted for FLT3-ITD models)
additional_notes: Listed as clinical/approved in some regions; strong activity in FLT3-ITD models

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and AXL signaling to reduce AML cell survival/proliferation
additional_notes: Described as approved in AML; relevant comparator; MOLM-13 commonly responsive

mechanism: Inhibits RAF kinases and receptor tyrosine kinases including VEGFR and FLT3
additional_notes: Approved for solid tumors; noted off-label use in FLT3-ITD AML studies

mechanism: Inhibits multiple RTKs including FLT3, KIT, and VEGFR
additional_notes: Approved; noted anti-proliferative in some AML contexts

mechanism: Inhibits SRC family kinases and ABL to disrupt survival signaling in some AML subsets
additional_notes: Approved; text notes reported AML effects in subsets

mechanism: Broad kinase inhibition including FLT3 variants
additional_notes: Approved; noted experimental use in AML

mechanism: Inhibits PI3Kδ to reduce PI3K/AKT pathway signaling
additional_notes: Approved; PI3K/AKT axis noted as often active in AML

mechanism: Inhibits PI3Kα and PI3Kδ to block downstream PI3K/AKT signaling
additional_notes: Approved; noted as sometimes more broadly cytotoxic than δ-selective PI3K inhibitors

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1/2 to suppress JAK/STAT signaling
additional_notes: Approved; JAK/STAT implicated in AML microenvironment and subsets

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2 to reduce MAPK pathway signaling downstream of FLT3/RAS
additional_notes: Approved; MAPK pathway noted as engaged downstream of FLT3/RAS

mechanism: Inhibits mTORC1 to reduce growth/proliferation signaling
additional_notes: Approved; noted modest single-agent effects but testable

mechanism: Inhibits mTOR signaling to reduce proliferation/survival pathways
additional_notes: Approved; described as similar to everolimus with potentially different cellular potency

mechanism: Inhibits CDK4/6 to block cell-cycle progression (G1 arrest)
additional_notes: Approved; AML sensitivity described as variable

mechanism: Inhibits CDK4/6; noted additional off-target CDK9/transcriptional effects
additional_notes: Approved; text notes more CDK9/off-target transcriptional impact vs palbociclib; sometimes stronger cytotoxicity

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to alter chromatin/transcription and promote anti-leukemic effects
additional_notes: Approved; noted often potent in vitro

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to modulate gene expression
additional_notes: Approved; described as broader HDAC inhibitor and typically less potent than panobinostat

mechanism: Exhibits HDAC-inhibitory activity; used in differentiation/epigenetic regimens
additional_notes: Approved; higher concentrations needed per text; widely tested in AML differentiation/epigenetic regimens

mechanism: Inhibits lysosomal acidification/autophagic flux to impair autophagy
additional_notes: Approved; noted can sensitize AML; variable single-agent cytotoxicity

mechanism: Inhibits lysosomal function/autophagic flux similarly to chloroquine
additional_notes: Approved; described as similar to chloroquine with typically slightly higher IC50s
